items.title: This tax season is a key time to delete your personal data from the internet
items.summary: It’s never been easier for spammers and scammers to get access to your personal data, and this is particularly dangerous during tax season. With data brokers providing easy access to your phone number, email address, physical address – and even sensitive data like your social security number – tax season can be peak time for fraud. Fortunately, there’s a simple way to reduce the risk. more…

items.title: This is what a fan is supposed to be — bladeless, sleek and customizable
items.summary: The Shark TurboBlade Fan is a sleek, quiet, highly customizable bladeless fan that cools efficiently without the noise, and it’s more than half off. (via Cult of Mac - Your source for the latest Apple news, rumors, analysis, reviews, how-tos and deals. )

items.title: Today in Apple history: Apple racks up staggering $700 million loss
items.summary: On March 28, 1996, Apple warned Wall Street that it would report a $700 million quarterly loss. It was Apple's worst quarter ever. (via Cult of Mac - Your source for the latest Apple news, rumors, analysis, reviews, how-tos and deals. )

items.title: You Do Not, in Fact, Have to Hand It to Them
Improved Git Diffs with Delta, Fzf and a Little Shell Scripting
Bacteria invent another way to turn on genes
items.summary: In their landmark 1961 paper on the lac operon, Nobel laureates François Jacob and Jacques Monod speculated that RNA might control gene activity in bacteria through base-pairing interactions. But once protein transcription factors were discovered, the idea was tossed aside. Sixty years later, a multi-institutional team of biologists shows that Jacob and Monod were on to something. Some species of bacteria, reports the team in Nature, have evolved an RNA-guided gene activating system by transform
items.source_name: Phys.org - latest science and technology news stories

items.title: Giant craters may reveal if Psyche is a lost planetary core
items.summary: When we think of asteroids, we almost immediately think of giant rocks bouncing around like the iconic chase scene in "The Empire Strikes Back," and we often hear how they are remnants from the birth of the solar system. While the asteroids that comprise the Main Asteroid Belt of our solar system are not only spread far apart from one another, they are also not all made of rock. One asteroid approximately the size of the state of Massachusetts called 16 Psyche is made of metal, which planetary s

items.title: College students are writing with AI, but a pilot study finds they're not simply letting it write for them
items.summary: Debates about generative AI in higher education have been informed by studies of completed student papers, or self-reported survey data. Research shows that artificial intelligence tools can support learning, but has also raised concerns, including students' overreliance, cheating, and the potential degradation of critical thinking and engagement.

items.title: He Helped Write the Clean Air Act. He Fears for Its Future.
items.summary: Thomas Jorling, adviser to Republicans who cosponsored the 1970 law, disputes the Trump administration’s claim that it shouldn’t apply to planet-warming greenhouse gases.

items.title: Are you addicted to technology? 6 questions experts use to help spot red flags
items.summary: A landmark court ruling has many asking whether their own tech use should raise red flags. Clinicians in the field suggest asking yourself a few brutally honest questions.

items.title: As stocks and bonds fall, and oil hits $100, a futures trade that boomed in 2022 may again be a winner
items.summary: Managed futures, which track macro themes expected to play out over months, do well when traditional assets are falling. This could be one of those times.
